journal-title-group.journal-title: Indian Journal of Urology : IJU : Journal of the Urological Society of India

title-group.article-title: Impact of modern urology and greenhouse emissions on the environment and how can we reduce it

article-meta.aff: 1Head of the Department of Urology, BAPS Hospital, Vadodara, Gujarat, India

license.license-p: This is an open access journal, and articles are distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 License, which allows others to remix, tweak, and build upon the work non-commercially, as long as appropriate credit is given and the new creations are licensed under the identical terms.

sec.p: Received 2024 Dec 12; Revised 2025 Jan 7; Accepted 2025 Apr 13; Issue date 2025 Jul-Sep.

body.p: Climate change attributable to global warming is disrupting the balance of nature. Greenhouse gases occur naturally in the atmosphere and are essential for our survival. Human activities are the culprit for its accumulation in abnormal proportion and consequential deleterious effects. Health care accounts for 4%–5% of greenhouse emissions.[1] Hospitals, mostly air conditioned, require 24-h electricity supply. Urology is a technology-driven specialty where many high-energy devices leave behind a heavy carbon footprint. Furthermore, healthcare facilities produce a large amount of plastic and biomedical waste.

Many diseases have a direct correlation with global warming. For example, the incidence of urolithiasis is expected to increase further in the coming years.[2] There are a multitude of aspects by which urologists can modify practice to endorse the environment.

sec.title: HEALTHCARE MODIFICATIONS
sec.p: Inhalational general anesthetic agents are responsible for greenhouse emissions,[3] desflurane being the most harmful. All attempts must be made for their proper disposal and ensure they are not vented in the air. As compared to general anesthesia, local and spinal/regional anesthesia have a lesser impact on the environment. Percutaneous nephrolithotomy, often carried out under general anesthesia can also be done safely and effectively under spinal anesthesia.[4] Whenever feasible, regional anesthesia should be contemplated without generalizations.

Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy has taken a back seat with the advent of newer modalities. However, it obviates the use of anesthesia in adults, uses less disposables, has good efficacy and safety, and, therefore, should be considered in properly selected patients.[5]

Disposable endoscopes come with a noteworthy offering of greenhouse emissions 24–47 times that of reusable scopes.[6] A pragmatic approach, where detrimental effect on the environment does not outweigh the aseptic benefit, should be followed.[7] With good sterilization techniques, reusable scopes can continue to serve a substantial number of patients.

Imaging modalities such as computed tomography and magnetic resonance imaging scans should be handled prudently. Rather than doing them as a protocol for certain urological diseases, we can contemplate using them only when necessary.[8,9,10]

Robotic surgery has come in a big way for urological procedures in the last two decades. We should not be prejudiced for using it and make sure that the carbon footprint remains “as low as reasonably achievable.”[11]
Urologists should use eco-friendly methods which have assisted us in the past. For example, sterile water in autoclavable cans can be used for irrigation.[12]

Virtual clinics have come in a big way, ever since COVID-19 restricted our mobility.[13] This novel concept promises to have multitudinous benefits saving time, money, and the ecosystem. The importance of clinical evaluation cannot be understated, nonetheless, to begin-with, the follow-up visits of stable patients can be reduced by virtual clinics.

sec.title: AWARENESS
sec.p: Social media is a boon, and a succinctly conveyed message can have a humongous effect. Urology Earth is a WhatsApp group of Indian urologists formed in May 2023. It has more than 130 members, and it is heartening to see the efforts put in by colleagues for the development and preservation of nature. Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle is an exemplary policy for waste management which should be inculcated universally.

Scientific conferences are another major contributor to greenhouse effect. With an increasing number of scientific societies, the number of conferences has increased tremendously for the last few years. Ways to reduce carbon footprint include organizing hybrid events, shared accommodation and travel, using halls with natural lights, and reducing food waste.

sec.title: UNDERSTANDING LUXURY VIS-A-VIS NECESSITY
sec.p: Science and technology are advancing swiftly; however, we must be cognizant of its consequences on environment. Air conditioners were designed to produce soothing ambience. Ironically, they protect us from heat by producing more heat in the atmosphere. Their rampant exhibition be it our offices, homes and cars are not justified. We may afford it monetarily but the big question is, can we afford it environmentally? In any hospital, the operation theaters consume the most amount of electricity. When not in use, the air conditioners should be shut down. Let us pledge to utilize this resource rather than exploit it.

Transportation sector is responsible for producing large amount of greenhouse gases. Use of public transport, carpooling and timely servicing of vehicles is advisable to curtail pollution. Electric vehicles seem safer for the environment and reduce pollution by over two-thirds during car’s lifespan.[14] This benefit remains substantial even after considering production and maintenance facets. We must consider cycling and walking down to our workplace if we live in vicinity.

Solar energy is in abundance in India, and efforts must be made to take full advantage of it. Both rural and urban sectors can revolutionize the effectiveness of biogas through proper sewage management. Wind energy and hydropower can be explored further to reduce the use of fossil fuels.
The government should also strengthen the regulatory bodies and try ensuring proper waste disposal and other measures to protect the environment.

sec.title: CONCLUSION
sec.p: Task to tackle greenhouse emissions may look insurmountable. Whenever the demon of any problem looks huge, remember it is not too hard to hit, rather it is too hard to miss. Positive changes in the environment by our constant efforts will be slow and gradual. Let urologists lead the way and leave the footsteps for others to follow. We must be clairvoyant to see the plight of the nature and not wait for the natural disasters to shake us. We should heed the warnings, before it is too late.

sec.title: Conflicts of interest
sec.p: There are no conflicts of interest.
